segments.title: What is photosynthesis?: intuition: intuition
segments.content: Start with the big idea: photosynthesis. Focus on why it matters before adding detail.

segments.title: What is photosynthesis?: intuition: core idea
segments.content: Now add the core detail: the supporting detail. Start with the big idea: photosynthesis. Focus on why it matters before adding detail.

segments.title: What is photosynthesis?: core idea
segments.content: Now add the core detail: the supporting detail. Photosynthesis is how plants make food.

segments.title: Plants need sunlight
segments.content: Plants use sunlight to create energy.

segments.title: OVERLOAD: All concepts at once
segments.content: Chlorophyll captures photons, water splits, oxygen leaves, ATP carries energy, carbon fixation uses carbon dioxide, glucose stores energy, light reactions happen in thylakoids, dark reactions in stroma, photosystem I and II, electron transport chains, and NADPH.

segments.title: The equation
segments.content: The chemical equation is 6CO2 + 6H2O + light → C6H12O6 + 6O2.

segments.title: Analogy
segments.content: Think of photosynthesis like a solar panel: it converts light energy into stored energy.
